message:
feat(workspace): extract frankenterm-core-resource-types leaf crate (ft-usvnt)

Tier-1 leaf crate carved out below frankenterm-core to break the
fleet → core back-edge for resource-tier reads (ft-y0loj.3.1). Holds
backpressure.rs (capture/storage queue-depth FSM) and backpressure_severity.rs
(continuous-severity throttling), both of which had zero first-party
imports and so are clean leaf material.

Mechanics:
- Created crates/frankenterm-core-resource-types/ with serde/serde_json/
  thiserror/tracing as its only dependencies.
- git mv'd the two .rs files; preserved every internal `crate::backpressure::*`
  call site by adding `pub use frankenterm_core_resource_types::{backpressure,
  backpressure_severity};` to frankenterm-core/src/lib.rs.
- Added the crate as a workspace member and wired it into both
  frankenterm-core (path-dep) and frankenterm-core-fleet (path-dep).

Build verification (CC=llvm-clang, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-cc6-target):
- cargo check -p frankenterm-core-resource-types: 3.05s clean
- cargo check -p frankenterm-core: 1m07s clean (warnings only)
- cargo check -p frankenterm-core-fleet: 1m15s clean
- cargo check --workspace: 1m33s clean (only pre-existing deprecation warnings)
- All 32 in-tree tests + benches that import frankenterm_core::backpressure::*
  resolve transparently via the re-export.

NOT extracted in this pass (deliberate — see lib.rs doc):
- memory_budget.rs and memory_pressure.rs were initially staged but reverted.
  Their Manager types call crate::cx::Cx, crate::runtime_compat::sleep_with_cx,
  and crate::outcome::CancelKind — all of which live in higher-tier
  frankenterm-core modules. Promoting the whole files here would either pull
  cx/runtime_async/outcome down too (large) or require splitting each .rs
  into a leaf "tier types" shard plus a core-resident "manager" shard
  (surgical, follow-up bead).

Fleet 3-module re-extraction status (ft-y0loj.3 stretch goal):
- Still blocked. Even with BackpressureTier reachable from a leaf crate,
  fleet_launcher / fleet_memory_controller / fleet_scrollback_coordinator
  also use BudgetLevel + MemoryPressureTier (still in core), and 4 in-tree
  importers (runtime, unified_telemetry, tx_execution, mission_agent_mail)
  consume the fleet types substantively. The fleet → core back-edge via
  unified_telemetry remains; making core → fleet a dep would still cycle.
- Real unblocker: split memory_budget/memory_pressure into leaf-tier shards
  (this bead's follow-up) AND extract unified_telemetry to a leaf crate
  (separate bead). Then fleet stops needing core entirely.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -0,0 +1,18 @@
+[package]
+name = "frankenterm-core-resource-types"
+description = "Shared resource primitives (backpressure / memory budget / memory pressure) extracted from frankenterm-core (ft-y0loj.3.1, ft-usvnt)"
+version.workspace = true
+edition.workspace = true
+rust-version.workspace = true
+authors.workspace = true
+license-file.workspace = true
+repository.workspace = true
+
+[lints]
+workspace = true
+
+[dependencies]
+serde = { workspace = true }
+serde_json = { workspace = true }
+thiserror = { workspace = true }
+tracing = { workspace = true }

@@ -0,0 +1,33 @@
+//! `frankenterm-core-resource-types` — shared resource primitives (ft-usvnt / ft-y0loj.3.1).
+//!
+//! Tier-1 leaf crate sitting **below** `frankenterm-core`. Holds the load-bearing
+//! resource primitives consumed by *both* `frankenterm-core` and
+//! `frankenterm-core-fleet`.
+//!
+//! Extracting these here breaks the symmetry that previously forced
+//! `frankenterm-core-fleet` to depend on `frankenterm-core` *just* to read a
+//! `BackpressureTier` enum, which in turn made `frankenterm-core →
+//! frankenterm-core-fleet` a cargo cycle (see
+//! `docs/proposals/ft-l3tfo-cold-build-measurements.md`).
+//!
+//! Modules:
+//! - [`backpressure`] — capture/storage queue-depth FSM (Green / Yellow / Red / Black).
+//! - [`backpressure_severity`] — continuous-severity throttling function (sigmoid).
+//!
+//! ## Modules NOT extracted in this pass (and why)
+//!
+//! `memory_budget` and `memory_pressure` were initially staged for inclusion but
+//! reverted: their `Manager` types call `crate::cx::Cx::current()`,
+//! `crate::runtime_compat::sleep_with_cx`, and `crate::outcome::CancelKind` —
+//! all of which live in higher-tier `frankenterm-core` modules. Promoting the
+//! whole files here would require either (a) extracting `cx`/`runtime_async`/
+//! `outcome` first, or (b) splitting each `.rs` file into a leaf "tier types"
+//! shard plus a core-resident "manager" shard. Both are valid follow-ups and
+//! tracked under ft-y0loj.3.1.next.
+//!
+//! Anything pulled into this crate that needs `crate::cx`, `crate::runtime_async`,
+//! or other higher-tier modules must stay in `frankenterm-core` for now and
+//! grow a thin trait at this layer instead.
+
+pub mod backpressure;
+pub mod backpressure_severity;

@@ -24,6 +24,7 @@ tracing.workspace = true
 tracing-subscriber.workspace = true
 futures.workspace = true
 frankenterm-alloc.workspace = true
+frankenterm-core-resource-types = { path = "../frankenterm-core-resource-types" }
 
 # Pattern matching
 aho-corasick.workspace = true
@@ -127,6 +128,10 @@ insta = { version = "1.43", features = ["json", "redactions"] }
 # import the lexical-search modules from the new sub-crate. This is a
 # dev-only edge — production code does NOT depend on the sub-crate.
 frankenterm-core-tantivy = { path = "../frankenterm-core-tantivy" }
+# ft-y0loj.2 / ft-mr35k: proptest_ars_*.rs integration tests import from
+# the new ars sub-crate. Cargo allows dev-dep cycles; production code
+# does NOT depend on this sub-crate.
+frankenterm-core-ars = { path = "../frankenterm-core-ars" }
 
 # Benchmarks
 [[bench]]
